title: Living in Boulder blog 8-6-2017: Gregory the Great & Marcellinus

enrichment.summary: Kyle updates viewers on his personal history education project, explaining he spent two days working on reading/organizing history books up through the 600s AD, including works on Gregory the Great and Marcellinus. He shares ideas for visualizing his reading progress as a formation of soldiers on the Roman forum, and mentions wanting to build a map of Rome's 14 Augustan regions using Google Earth.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: tempted to expand but worried it could get out of control
should stay focused on content already on his website rather than branching out

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done a log for two days because I was working on history books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought I was almost done with history books up until the fall of Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I did some books covering the period up until the 600s, after the fall of Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I compiled a collection of Rome-related books totaling 3,200 pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked on a book about Marcelline but had to rebuild it because it was wrong.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I watched YouTube videos by Ryan Reeves about religious history to learn about the church fathers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Ryan Reeves mentioned figures like Basil the Great that weren't on my list, so I want to add them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's tempting to collect more books throughout history, but I could get out of control.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think it would be cool to have the Magna Carta in my collection.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to organize my education materials and group them by a symbolic representation, like letters or numbers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I imagine arranging the books like soldiers in formation, placed on top of the Roman Forum.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought of making the height of the representations correspond to the number of pages in each book.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to create a visual system that makes memorizing all the material feel achievable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was listening to Ryan Reeves while working on books about Marcelline and Danielos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll start writing again when I get my pedals back.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I don't get my pedals soon, I'll continue working on my boat projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'd like to map the 14 regions of Augusta, but there isn't a good existing map.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found a map showing the 14 regions of Italy, but I might have to build one on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to base the map on the book 'The Platinum Ash' from my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I bought everything related to 'The Platinum Ash' to organize the buildings I've built.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel like this project would bring me closer to completing my goals, but I should stay focused.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I still have a little work left to do, so I should prioritize that.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to sleep now.
